entities.canonical_name: National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations
entities.evidence_text: Official NIDHI information identifies NIDHI as DST's umbrella programme and lists its permanent startup-support components.

entities.canonical_name: NIDHI - Promoting and Accelerating Young and Aspiring Innovators and Startups
entities.evidence_text: The official master page explicitly describes NIDHI-PRAYAS as sector-agnostic and explains startup and innovator access through approved centres.
Official NIDHI material identifies EIR as a permanent component that reduces early-stage risk for aspiring technology entrepreneurs.
The official SSP page describes seed assistance to potential startups through supported incubators and distinguishes incubator calls from the permanent programme.
The official page separates institutional calls to establish TBIs from startup intake conducted by individual TBIs.
Official NIDHI information identifies iTBI as a permanent NIDHI component and a delivery institution for inclusive startup support.
The official page explicitly states that supported accelerator programmes may be sector-specific or sector-agnostic; individual cohort sectors must be stored on calls.
The official NIDHI component list identifies CoE as a permanent component for globally competitive startup facilities.
RDIF is the permanent DST flagship fund under ANRF. It finances private-sector enterprises and startups in sunrise and strategic sectors through designated Second-Level Fund Managers such as TDB.

Official TDB material describes continuing core technology-commercialisation finance. RDIF financing through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager is tracked separately under the Research Development and Innovation Fund.

monitored_call_pages.call_title: 1st Open Call under RDI Fund through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager

current_call_overrides.call_title: 1st Open Call under RDI Fund through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager
current_call_overrides.status_evidence: The official TDB RDIF-SLFM page displays an active Apply Now link to the RDIF registration portal; the TDB page was last updated on 08/07/2026.
current_call_overrides.status_reason: Open status is supported by the active official Apply Now route and operational RDIF registration form; no closing date is published.
current_call_overrides.startup_relevance_reason: The official TDB page explicitly includes DPIIT-defined startups among eligible technology entities receiving RDIF support through SLFMs.
current_call_overrides.applicant_layer_reason: Eligible Indian technology entities, including DPIIT-recognised startups at TRL 4 and above, apply through TDB's RDIF registration route.
current_call_overrides.parent_resolution_reason: RDIF is the permanent fund; TDB is its designated Second-Level Fund Manager for this application route.
current_call_overrides.secondary_sectors: ClimateTech & Clean Energy; Artificial Intelligence & Data; Biotechnology & Medical Technologies; Digital Economy & AgriTech; Space Technology; Semiconductors & Electronics
current_call_overrides.sector_reason: The official RDIF/TDB pages enumerate sunrise and strategic sectors including clean energy, quantum, robotics, space, AI, agriculture, health, biotechnology, medical devices and the digital economy.
current_call_overrides.eligible_applicants: Indian legal entities including DPIIT-defined startups, companies, partnerships and LLPs; resident-Indian control, Indian headquarters and RDI-intensive technology at TRL 4 or above are required
current_call_overrides.funding_summary: Long-tenure collateral-free loan, equity or hybrid support; normally up to 50% of assessed project cost. No grant funding. TDB states 3%–4% interest and a 12–15 year period for RDIF loans.
current_call_overrides.evidence_note: Curated from official DST, RDIF/ANRF and TDB sources on 2026-07-11.
current_call_overrides.source_evidence: Official sources establish RDIF as the parent fund, TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager, startup eligibility, TRL 4+ requirement, multi-sector scope, financing terms and an active RDIF application route.

current_call_overrides.call_title: Call for Proposals on Building U.S.-India Partnerships to Drive Innovation in Critical Minerals and Quantum Technology
current_call_overrides.status_evidence: Official DST page states that online submission is open until 31 July 2026.
current_call_overrides.startup_relevance_reason: The current official page explicitly seeks S&T-based entrepreneurial initiatives beyond ideation with commercialization potential.
current_call_overrides.applicant_layer_reason: The official call directly seeks joint S&T-based entrepreneurial initiatives.
current_call_overrides.parent_resolution_reason: USISTEF is the implementing programme; it is not yet represented as a curator-approved permanent parent identity.
current_call_overrides.sector_reason: The official current call explicitly limits proposals to quantum technologies and critical minerals/supply-chain resilience.
current_call_overrides.eligible_applicants: Joint U.S.-India S&T-based entrepreneurial initiatives beyond ideation stage
current_call_overrides.funding_summary: Grant-in-aid up to INR 1.5 crore per project for up to 24 months.
current_call_overrides.evidence_note: Curated from the official DST page on 2026-07-11.
current_call_overrides.source_evidence: Official DST text states that the call supports promising joint U.S.-India S&T-based entrepreneurial initiatives, offers up to INR 1.5 crore per project, and is open until 31 July 2026.

current_call_overrides.status_evidence: Official DST and TDB pages state that the call is open from 1 April 2026 until 28 August 2026.
current_call_overrides.startup_relevance_reason: Eligible Indian applicants are for-profit Indian companies, which can include incorporated startups, but the official call does not explicitly designate startups as a beneficiary class.
current_call_overrides.applicant_layer_reason: The official call addresses Indian companies; startup applicability requires curator confirmation.
current_call_overrides.parent_resolution_reason: TDB implements this bilateral call, but implementation alone does not prove that TDB Core Funding is its permanent parent.
current_call_overrides.secondary_sectors: Artificial Intelligence & Data; Electronics & Semiconductors; Mobility & Electric Vehicles
current_call_overrides.sector_reason: The official TDB page lists sustainable energy, advanced digital technologies, microelectronics/power electronics and electric vehicles among the call priorities.
current_call_overrides.eligible_applicants: Indian for-profit companies registered under the Companies Act, subject to ownership and capability requirements
current_call_overrides.funding_summary: Up to INR 2.5 crore or 50 percent of Indian project cost, whichever is lower, as a grant.
current_call_overrides.evidence_note: Curated from official DST and TDB pages on 2026-07-11; startup applicability requires human confirmation.
current_call_overrides.source_evidence: Official DST and TDB pages state the 1 April to 28 August 2026 window, Indian-company eligibility, thematic priorities, TDB implementation and grant ceiling.
